title: Korea Tightens Policy Coordination Among Government, Central Bank, Regulators
summary: South Korea's government convenes expanded macroeconomic meeting to enhance coordination on financial stability. Deputy Prime Minister Koo Yun-cheol leads discussions with Bank of Korea Governor and financial chiefs. Participants commit to closer collaboration on forex market reforms and structural improvements.
contents:
Koo Yun-cheol, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Strategy and Finance, convened an expanded macroeconomic and financial meeting on April 30, 2026. Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun-song, Financial Services Commission Chairman Lee Eog-weon, and Financial Supervisory Service Governor Lee Chan-jin attended at the Korea Federation of Banks in Jung-gu, Seoul. Koo announced plans to develop enhanced cooperation channels for macroprudential soundness and financial issues.

Participants agreed to coordinate closely with financial institutions. They prepare for 24-hour foreign exchange market opening and offshore won settlement system establishment. Discussions emphasized accelerating structural reforms in forex and financial markets.

The meeting also addressed fiscal, public, and regulatory institutional reforms. Government officials pledged tighter policy alignment amid economic pressures. This initiative responds to ongoing volatility in global markets.

Koo stressed continued monitoring of financial landscapes. Authorities aim to ensure seamless implementation of market enhancements. The coordination strengthens South Korea's financial resilience.

Financial leaders committed to proactive measures. They target improved efficiency and stability in operations. This framework supports broader economic policy objectives.
